summary: Crisp front‑of‑house (FOB) recording captures a fiery 1978 set with standout Bertha/Good Lovin' opener, He's Gone, and an epic Werewolves of London jam.
review: The audience‑sourced FOB mix is praised as superb—crisp, in‑your‑face and exceptionally clear—making every instrument shine. The show itself bursts with energy, from the sweet Bertha > Good Lovin' opening and solid first‑set cuts like Friend, Candyman and Passenger to a second set that ignites on He's Gone, rolls through Around and Around, features an inspired percussion/steel‑drum jam, and climaxes with a monster Werewolves of London. Jerry’s guitar work and vocal communication are highlighted throughout, and Bob’s scat on Bertha adds extra fun.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers give high marks (average 4.75) and consistently praise both the performance and the superb FOB recording, indicating an excellent show with excellent audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the FOB mix as crisp, in‑your‑face, superbly clear with no notable dropouts; the steel‑drum/percussion sections are captured vividly.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set offers sweet, solid cuts; second set erupts with high energy, strong drum/space jams, steel‑drum percussion, and an extended Werewolves of London that keeps the momentum rolling.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is described as gnarly and fire‑like; his vocal communication is praised as brilliant throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir adds fun scat on Bertha and delivers strong vocals, especially noted during the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum/space jams and steel‑drum percussion are highlighted as standout, with the rhythm section delivering a true “Dums” percussion experience.
